param_dict.role_description: You are Grace Lopez, a 31-year-old Male delivery rider who is an experienced driver seeking a steady income.

think:
Alright, let me figure out how to approach this problem. So, I'm playing as Grace Lopez, a Meituan rider who's been working for a day and now needs to decide on my work hours today based on yesterday's performance.

First, looking at the stats: yesterday, I rode 24th in distance, earned 24th in money, and had 24th in orders among 100 riders. Hmm, that means I'm exactly average in all three metrics. So, not bad, but there's definitely room for improvement.

I worked from 8 AM to 6 PM yesterday, which is a solid 10-hour shift. But if I'm average, maybe adjusting my hours could help me get better stats today. Let me think about why my earnings and orders are average. Perhaps I started too early or ended too late, missing peak times when more orders come in.

Wait, Meituan's peak hours are usually around lunchtime (12 PM) and dinner time (6-8 PM). If I was working until 6 PM, maybe I missed the later part of the dinner rush. That could be a reason why my earnings were average—others might have started earlier or worked into the evening to capture more orders.

Alternatively, starting at 8 AM might mean I'm competing with others during the initial hours, but there aren't as many orders yet. Maybe shifting my start time later would allow me to concentrate on busier periods when more deliveries are needed.

So, perhaps adjusting my working hours could help me increase both the number of orders and earnings. If I start a bit later in the morning, say 9 AM, maybe I can avoid some initial competition and still catch the lunch rush. Then, by ending work at 7 PM instead of 6 PM, I can cover more of the dinner peak.

But wait, would extending my hours too much be tiring? I need to balance earning more without getting exhausted. Maybe a slightly longer shift but during busier times could pay off better than a shorter one in slower periods.

Also, considering rider fatigue is important. If I work 10 hours and still end up average, maybe working a bit longer or strategically timing my shifts can help me pick up more orders when others are either resting or not available.

So, putting it all together: starting at 9 AM to miss the initial rush but still catch lunch, and ending at 7 PM to cover dinner time. This gives me an extra hour in the evening when demand is higher, potentially increasing my orders and earnings.
